draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer snappy, click-based contact mode switching over animated transitions between client views
facade.label: Instant contact switch
facade.acceptImplies: User values speed and directness in navigation; likely prefers crisp interactions and minimal motion overhead
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer fluid transitions and visual continuity when moving between contacts; values motion as wayfinding aid

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer approachable, non-corporate interfaces that feel less intimidating than traditional CRM tools
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward friendly, accessible visual language; softer aesthetic may signal less formal, more human-centered design direction
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers crisp, professional geometry; may indicate preference for authority, clarity, and traditional corporate visual language

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer snappy, click-based contact mode switching over animated transitions between client views
evidence.implication: User values speed and directness in navigation; likely prefers crisp interactions and minimal motion overhead

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to interfaces that feel personal and established rather than sleek and new
facade.label: Warm, lived-in
facade.acceptImplies: The CRM should use soft colors, subtle textures, and a sense of familiarity—like a space that's been carefully organized over time rather than freshly designed
facade.rejectImplies: The user may prefer crisp, modern, minimalist aesthetics that feel intentional and uncluttered—or they may want the interface to feel more professional/corporate than intimate

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to warm, approachable copy than corporate jargon in CRM interfaces
facade.label: Conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: Design voice should feel like talking to a trusted colleague; use contractions, casual language, encouragement. Error messages are helpful, not scolding.
facade.rejectImplies: Design voice should be formal and authoritative; use standard business language, professional distance. Clear hierarchy between system and user.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need persistent access to their client roster and will prefer a fixed sidebar over tab-based navigation
facade.label: Sidebar with client list
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick client switching and persistent context; sidebar-first IA likely matches workflow
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer cleaner main canvas, tabbed switching, or card-based client views; tabs or modal access might suit better

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers want a quick-scan status board (charts, KPIs, numbers) rather than a scrolling list of client updates and project news
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data visualization vs. storytelling through sequential information
facade.label: Dashboard or narrative feed
facade.acceptImplies: Show metrics-first design: revenue charts, project completion %s, pipeline summaries, revenue-per-client cards arranged in a grid. Numbers before stories.
facade.rejectImplies: Show activity-feed design: client messaged, invoice sent, project milestone reached—a timeline-like stream of events and updates in prose or minimal cards. Narrative over numbers.

axes.label: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels energetic and branded
Whether they favor dense information dashboards vs. progressive disclosure—reveals if they want everything visible at once or prefer guided, step-by-step navigation
Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/supportive tone—reveals if they want business-like efficiency or human encouragement
Whether they prefer calm, minimal atmosphere vs. energetic, motivational atmosphere—reveals if they want zen focus or inspiring momentum
Whether they want client data front-and-center vs. hidden behind filters—reveals if they prioritize quick scanning or organized, searchable structure
Whether they expect instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered transitions—reveals if they value speed or polish

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels energetic and branded
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they favor dense information dashboards vs. progressive disclosure—reveals if they want everything visible at once or prefer guided, step-by-step navigation
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/supportive tone—reveals if they want business-like efficiency or human encouragement
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer calm, minimal atmosphere vs. energetic, motivational atmosphere—reveals if they want zen focus or inspiring momentum
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they want client data front-and-center vs. hidden behind filters—reveals if they prioritize quick scanning or organized, searchable structure
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they expect instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered transitions—reveals if they value speed or polish
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer gesture-based navigation (swipe transitions) over click-based switching when managing multiple client contexts, contradicting the initial hesitant acceptance of instant contact switching
facade.axisTargeted: gesture vs click; fluid motion vs snappy directness
facade.label: Swipe to slide
facade.acceptImplies: User values fluid, continuous motion and gesture-based interaction for frequent navigation tasks; willing to trade some snappiness for spatial continuity and embodied control
facade.rejectImplies: User confirms preference for click-based, instantaneous switching; finds swiping unnecessary friction; CRM navigation should prioritize speed and directness over gesture elegance

draft.title: Freelancer CRM
draft.summary: A lightweight client and project management tool designed for independent freelancers to track leads, manage projects, and maintain client relationships.
